title: 2D TEM CLEM (Correlative Light Microscopy and Electron Microscopy)

steps: Culture cells on poly-d-lysine-coated 35 mm MatTek dish (P35G-1.5-14-CGRD) and transfect the cells with the plasmids of the interest.
Pre-fix the cells in 37 °C -warmed 4% PFA and 0.25% glutaraldehyde in the imaging buffer.
Wash in the same imaging buffer.

Image with a fluorescence microscopy, find the regions of the interest and their coordinates on the dish, then take the Z-stack fluorescence images and the grids map/coordinate by using phase contrast.
Fix the cells in 2.5% glutaraldehyde and 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer for 01:00:00 .
Wash in 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer.
Fix in 2% OsO 4 and 1.5% K 4 Fe(CN) 6 (Sigma-Aldrich) in 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer for 01:00:00 .
En bloc stained with 2% aqueous uranyl acetate Overnight at 4 °C .
Add two drops of pure Epon into the center of the glass bottom of the culture dish.
Cell of interest were relocated based on the pre-recorded coordinates under a stereomicroscope.
Trim and cut ultrathin sections (50-60 nm) with microtome.
Observe the ultrathin sections in a electron microscope.
Wash 00:05:00 in the same imaging buffer. (1/3)
Wash 00:05:00 in the same imaging buffer. (2/3)
Wash 00:05:00 in the same imaging buffer. (3/3)
Wash in 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer for 00:05:00 . (1/4)
Wash in 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer for 00:05:00 . (2/4)
Wash in 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer for 00:05:00 . (3/4)
Wash in 0.1 Molarity (M) sodium cacodylate buffer for 00:05:00 . (4/4)
creator: Yumei Wu
